content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(ii) WARN — NSCameraUsageDescription is generic ("This app needs camera access.", Info.plist:8-9) and NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription is a placeholder "TODO" (Info.plist:10-11) with no location usage visible in the code.
Pierre: Apple rejects apps whose purpose strings don't tell users why a permission is needed; "TODO" is an instant metadata rejection and the camera string is boilerplate. The camera prompt is triggered by document scanning (ScannerView.swift:12), so write something like "SnapScan uses the camera to scan documents." For location, either remove the key entirely (no CoreLocation usage found) or explain the specific feature that needs it.
